channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Georgia islanders rushed to rescue survivors after dock walkway collapse that killed 7
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Orange life jackets bobbed in the water, where bystanders rushed to form a human chain for passing survivors to safety. Others shouldered the task of wrapping bodies in blankets and carrying them to shore.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Helicopter crashes in Houston, killing 4 and destroying a radio tower, officials say
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A helicopter crashed Sunday in Houston, killing four people on board and destroying a radio tower, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas helicopter crash leaves 4 dead, including child, after hitting radio tower
item.description: Authorities say there were multiple fatalities after a helicopter struck a radio tower and crashed in Houston’s Second Ward.
item.dc:creator: Christian Terry, Corley Peel, T.J. Parker, KPRC 2

item.title: How the 2025 Social Security COLA compares to recent years
item.description: A recent survey from the Motley Fool shows 50% of retirees have considered going back to work to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crash in Houston kills 4, topples radio tower, officials say
item.description: The private helicopter with four people on board, including a child, either struck a cable or the tower, Houston Police Chief J. Noe Diaz said at a news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Ohio officer to stand trial in 2020 shooting death of Andre Hill
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A white former Ohio police officer is set to go on trial Monday in the killing of Andre Hill, a Black man who was shot as he emerged from a garage holding a cellphone nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some Jewish voters in presidential swing states reconsider their longtime devotion to Democrats
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — For Rona Kaufman, the signs are everywhere that more Jews feel abandoned by the Democratic Party and may vote for Republican Donald Trump. It's in her Facebook feed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted by subpoena set to testify to lawmakers
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted after a last-ditch maneuver by lawmakers who believe Robert Roberson did not kill his 2-year-old daughter was expected to testify before a state House panel on Monday, four days after he had been scheduled to die by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees and Dodgers meet in World Series for 12th time in matchup of Broadway and Hollywood
item.description:
Broadway vs. Hollywood. Subway vs. Freeway. Judge vs. Ohtani.
New York neighbors who became cross-country rivals, the Yankees and Dodgers renew their starry struggle in the World Series for the first time in 43 years.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar brought heavy rains and winds to Cuba, an island already beleaguered by a massive power outage, late Sunday after brushing the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SEC fines Tennessee $100,000 for rushing the field after win over Alabama
item.description: While Tennessee was fined $100,000 when fans rushed the field after beating Alabama in 2022, this penalty is the school's first violation since the policy was revised last year.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden and first lady toast a reimagined White House tour that she's unveiling on Monday
item.description: President Joe Biden and first lady Jill Biden on Sunday toasted the unveiling of a long-awaited reimagined public White House tour.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Oscar makes landfall in eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: Hurricane Oscar has made landfall in eastern Cuba, an island beleaguered by a massive power outage, after striking the southeastern Bahamas. The National Hurricane Center in Miami says the storm's center arrived in the Cuban province of Guantanamo, near the city of Baracoa, on Sunday evening.
item.dc:creator: AP, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security benefits: What young people should know
item.description: The Social Security Administration continually tries to remind people of all ages that it pays benefit to more than just retirees.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, ABC 4

item.title: Texas' junk science law is getting another look over Robert Roberson's case
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — When Robert Roberson's execution was abruptly halted in Texas, it was due to a subpoena ordering him to testify over a legal backstop that both Republicans and Democrats say should had saved him long ago: Texas’ junk science law.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Calmer weather helps contain Oakland, California, fire that forced evacuations
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Firefighting crews took advantage of calmer weather Sunday to increase containment of a small wildfire in Oakland, California, that burned two homes and forced more than 500 people to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nebraska Rep. Don Bacon urges 'respect' for election
item.description: “We have to respect our elections, and I get it, candidates have the right to go to court and appeal, or to challenge versus … various processes,” Bacon told NewsNation’s Chris Stirewalt.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Raffensperger denies voter fraud amid Georgia’s historic early-vote turnout
item.description: “We’re going to respond quickly to these sorts of things in 2024 because it’s not supported by the facts,” Raffensperger said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend: Survey
item.description: According to NerdWallet’s annual holiday spending report, Americans are spending quite a bit more on holiday travel and gift shopping than last year.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, NewsNation

item.title: In New York, a constitutional amendment provides election fodder for the left and the right
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Democrats pushed to get a constitutional amendment on New York's ballot because they believed it could energize liberals eager to protect abortion rights.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stanford psychologist behind the controversial "Stanford Prison Experiment" dies at 91
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Philip G. Zimbardo, the psychologist behind the controversial “Stanford Prison Experiment” that was intended to examine the psychological experiences of imprisonment, has died. He was 91. Stanford University announced Friday that Zimbardo died Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indian government employee charged in foiled murder-for-hire plot in New York City
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department announced criminal charges Thursday against an Indian government employee who specialized in intelligence in connection with a foiled plot to kill a Sikh separatist leader living in New York City.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4
